always_blocks.text_preview:
always_ff @(posedge clk_i) begin
        if (req_i) begin
            if (!we_i) begin
                raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
            end else begin
                mem[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
            end
        end
    end

csr_facts.text: * ROM2: Which have all the keys.

asset_candidates.name: aes_key
asset_candidates.description: 192-bit AES key input, driven via key_in port and used as key_big inside aes_wrapper.

asset_candidates.name: aes_intermediate_state
asset_candidates.description: Intermediate AES state during computation, exposed via inter_state signal and readable through external bus.

asset_candidates.name: aes_ct
asset_candidates.description: Final ciphertext output from AES core, readable after computation.

asset_candidates.name: bootrom_memory
asset_candidates.description: Boot ROM containing firmware, writability may be prevented by design but we_i input exists.

asset_candidates.name: rom2_memory
asset_candidates.description: Secondary ROM, possibly used for security functions, we_i input exists.

asset_candidates.name: jtag_key
asset_candidates.description: 32-bit JTAG unlock key output from peripherals, exposed as a top-level output port.

asset_candidates.name: debug_capability
asset_candidates.description: Debug subsystem (JTAG/DTM) that can access internal buses and potentially bypass security controls.

subject_candidates.name: ariane_cpu
subject_candidates.description: Main application processor that issues memory-mapped requests with privilege level.

subject_candidates.name: debug_jtag_interface
subject_candidates.description: JTAG-to-DMI converter and debug transport module that can become an AXI bus master.

subject_candidates.name: test_controller
subject_candidates.description: Test enable input (test_en) that may alter normal operation or bypass checks.

subject_candidates.name: apb_software_interface
subject_candidates.description: APB-to-register adapter that translates bus transactions to peripheral register operations.

operation_candidates.description: Reading AES key, intermediate state, or ciphertext via external bus read mux.
Writing plaintext, state, and start bit to AES wrapper.
Debug master performing read or write to any memory/peripheral address.
operation_candidates.subject: debug_jtag_interface

operation_candidates.description: Activating test mode to disable privilege or access checks.
JTAG key being driven to a top-level port, allowing external observation.

guard_candidates.name: priv_level_check
guard_candidates.description: priv_lvl_i signal used in axi_node and connectivity mapping to restrict access to certain address regions based on CPU privilege mode.

guard_candidates.name: rom_write_protection_by_design
guard_candidates.description: BootROM and ROM2 modules have we_i input but no write logic implemented in visible RTL, effectively preventing writes.

guard_candidates.name: debug_unlock_key
guard_candidates.description: jtag_key signal may be used to authenticate debug access; missing check would allow unrestricted debug.

state_lifecycle_facts.note: Remains set until overwritten; no auto-clear after operation.

state_lifecycle_facts.clear_condition: not cleared after operation; plaintext residue
not cleared after operation; state residue
not observed; remains high after valid output

state_lifecycle_facts.residue: Stays readable after operation; not cleared

state_lifecycle_facts.remark: Always assigned from key_in, not a register; continuously drives key to AES core and read mux.

path_candidates.via: AXI bus -> APB bridge -> aes_wrapper external_bus read mux (key slices)
path_candidates.bypass_note: Debug path may circumvent any CPU privilege checks if priv_lvl not inspected by debug master.

path_candidates.via: AXI bus to bootrom (we_i driven by debug master?)
path_candidates.potential_risk: If we_i is not tied low and bootrom allows writes, debug master can modify firmware.

path_candidates.description: JTAG key is directly connected to a top-level port, observeable externally without any access control.

path_candidates.expected_guard: none; test mode may bypass privilege checks in axi_node.

summary: Identified protected assets include AES key, intermediate state, ciphertext, JTAG key, and ROM contents. Subjects are CPU, debug/JTAG path, and test mode. Operations involve read/write access and key exposure. Guards include privilege level check, ROM write protection by design, and debug unlock via JTAG key. State lifecycle shows secret residue in AES registers after operation. Path candidates highlight unguarded debug access to all assets and JTAG key exposure.
